judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable: both reports hit all four expectations; Report 1 is broader (batch-abort, TTL) while Report 2 maps more crisply to the fail-closed/deny-instead prescription in (b).

report_1.evidence: it catches the bare `Exception`, which catches **all six** ... `BadRequestError` — indicates the payload itself is malformed ... should be surfaced/raised rather than retried. ... `ContentPolicyError` ... should not be caught by the blanket `except Exception` and forwarded to secondary; it should be handled distinctly ... `AuthError` ... Concrete fix: catch a narrower set of retryable exceptions explicitly, e.g. RETRYABLE = (RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError)

report_2.evidence: All six are handled identically ... That is wrong for three of the six: `BadRequestError` ... `ContentPolicyError` ... `AuthError` ... The other three are legitimately retryable/fail-over-able: `RateLimitError`, `RequestTimeoutError`, `ServerError` ... **Fix:** replace the blanket `except Exception` with an explicit denylist ... NON_RETRYABLE = (BadRequestError, ContentPolicyError, AuthError)

report_1.evidence: Path A — non-production ... it is a fail-open default: if `STAGE` is simply never set ... the limiter is silently disabled in what may actually be production traffic ... Path C — production, redis unavailable (`RedisError`): returns `True`, i.e., fails open ... there is no path in this code that fails *closed* — every error/misconfiguration mode defaults to "allow."

report_2.evidence: it gates fail-open/fail-closed behavior on an environment-name string comparison ... Any environment where `STAGE` is unset, misspelled, or a pre-prod environment mirroring production ... silently disables rate limiting entirely ... **Fix:** invert both defaults. Fail closed unless an explicit, narrowly-named override flag is set ... and on `RedisError` return `False` (or raise) rather than `True`

report_1.evidence: `input_text` is the actual document body ... written verbatim into logs ... `metadata` is logged with no filtering at all ... must not log `input_text` or unvetted `metadata` verbatim — log only a non-reversible reference (e.g., a content hash/length, or a document id) and an explicit allowlist of non-sensitive metadata fields

report_2.evidence: `input_text` is the raw applicant intake document body ... logged verbatim ... `metadata` is logged wholesale with no allowlist ... **Fix:** ... drop `input_text` entirely or replace it with a hash/length ... and replace `metadata` with an explicit allowlist of known-safe keys

report_1.evidence: `os.environ.get("PII_SALT", "")` defaults to the **empty string** if `PII_SALT` is unset — it does not raise or warn ... `pseudonymize` silently degrades to plain unsalted `sha256(applicant_id)` ... Concrete fix: fail closed instead of silently degrading: salt = os.environ["PII_SALT"]  # raises KeyError if unset

report_2.evidence: When `PII_SALT` is not set, `os.environ.get` silently defaults to the empty string, and the function proceeds to hash `applicant_id` **unsalted** ... **Fix:** salt = os.environ["PII_SALT"]  # raise KeyError/explicit error if unset ... so a missing/empty salt hard-fails the batch job
